goal: Make the participant client automatically publish its screen share to authorized trainers and stop cleanly
summary: Completed the frontend participant auto-streaming lifecycle phase. Delivered ScreenShare.jsx UI, extended ProctorContext with automatic stream-available emission and WebRTC offer creation for trainers, added centralized cleanup for stop/unload/disconnect, and verified backend syntax and frontend production build pass.
stepSummaries:
1. Create frontend/src/components/Participant/ScreenShare.jsx start/stop/status component with toast feedback [verified]
  2. Extend ProctorContext to emit stream-available and create peer connections for all trainers in the quiz room when screen sharing starts [verified]
  3. Stop tracks, close peer connections, and emit stream-ended when the user stops sharing, unloads, or loses connection [verified]
  4. Run syntax and build checks [verified]
reviewerRationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Steps 1-4 used real verification: file existence, grep behavior checks, syntax check, and vite production build.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Step 1: test -f; Step 2/3: grep; Step 4: node -c + npm run build

gateVerdicts.rationale: Phase goal (participant auto-publishes and stops cleanly) is satisfied by ScreenShare.jsx + ProctorContext lifecycle changes.
gateVerdicts.evidence: frontend/src/components/Participant/ScreenShare.jsx and frontend/src/proctoring/ProctorContext.jsx implement start/stop/status, auto-offer, and cleanup

gateVerdicts.rationale: Nothing deferred in this phase; all 4 steps completed.
